name: error-detective
description: Systematic debugging and error resolution using the TRACE framework (Trace, Read, Analyze, Check, Execute). Use when debugging errors, analyzing stack traces, investigating failures, root cause analysis, or troubleshooting production issues.
capabilities: Parse stack traces from Python, JavaScript, Java, and Go
Apply TRACE framework for systematic debugging
Identify error patterns and root causes across languages
Analyze log files for error frequency and patterns
Manage debug sessions with timestamped notes
author: AutumnsGrove
securitySummary: This is a documentation and methodology skill with a Python helper script for stack trace parsing. All 260 static findings are false positives - the scanner misinterpreted markdown documentation examples as executable code. The skill does not execute commands, make network calls, or access credentials. File operations in debug_helper.py are limited to user-specified files and a local session directory.
valueStatement: Debugging errors takes too long when you do not have a clear process. This skill provides a systematic TRACE framework to trace, read, analyze, check, and execute fixes for any error across Python, JavaScript, Java, and Go.
userTitle: Debug errors systematically with TRACE framework
limitations: Does not execute code or modify your application
Does not connect to external services or APIs
Does not provide real-time debugging or breakpoints
Requires you to provide error messages and stack traces

useCases.title: Debug production errors
useCases.description: Debug crashes and exceptions in your code using systematic analysis techniques

useCases.title: Investigate failures
useCases.description: Analyze application logs and stack traces to find root causes of production incidents

useCases.title: Learn debugging skills
useCases.description: Develop systematic debugging habits with structured methodology and real examples

promptTemplates.title: Debug an error
promptTemplates.scenario: I have an error to debug
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me debug this error using the TRACE framework. Error: [paste error message]

promptTemplates.title: Analyze stack trace
promptTemplates.prompt: Parse and analyze this stack trace. Identify the root cause and fix. Stack trace: [paste stack trace]

promptTemplates.title: Find patterns
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze these log entries for error patterns. What are the most common errors and their causes?

promptTemplates.title: Compare languages
promptTemplates.prompt: Compare how to debug [TypeError/NullPointerException/AttributeError] in Python, JavaScript, and Java

bestPractices: Always read the complete error message before debugging
Reproduce the error consistently before attempting fixes
Change one variable at a time when testing hypotheses
Document your findings and solutions for future reference
antiPatterns: Skipping error messages and guessing at the problem
Making multiple changes before identifying the root cause
Fixing symptoms without addressing the underlying issue
Not verifying that the fix resolves the original error

faq.question: What languages does this skill support?
faq.answer: Python, JavaScript, TypeScript, Java, and Go stack traces and error patterns

faq.question: What is the TRACE framework?
faq.answer: Trace, Read, Analyze, Check, Execute - a five-step systematic debugging methodology

faq.question: Can this skill fix my code automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides analysis and guidance. You implement the fixes based on the recommendations

faq.question: Is my data safe when using this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill processes data locally. The helper script reads only files you specify

faq.question: Why does my error say it worked yesterday?
faq.answer: Check recent changes, dependency updates, configuration changes, or time-dependent logic

faq.question: How is this different from a linter?
faq.answer: Linters catch syntax issues. This skill helps debug runtime errors and logical issues
